import re

def test_azuread_group_mapping_settings_is_read_from_pipeline_config_not_legacy_setting__a1():
    """
    The fixed implementation no longer reads Azure AD group mapping from a legacy
    top-level Django setting SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP (via getattr/hasattr).
    Instead it requires SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG and reads AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP
    from that dict.

    This should FAIL on the "before" code (which uses SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP)
    and PASS on the "after" code (which uses SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG + AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    normalized = '\n'.join((line.rstrip() for line in source.splitlines()))
    assert 'SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG' in normalized, 'Expected azuread_map_groups to require/read SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG in the corrected implementation, but SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG was not found in /workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py.'

def test_azuread_group_mapping_settings_is_read_from_pipeline_config_not_legacy_setting__a2():
    """
    The fixed implementation no longer reads Azure AD group mapping from a legacy
    top-level Django setting SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP (via getattr/hasattr).
    Instead it requires SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG and reads AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP
    from that dict.

    This should FAIL on the "before" code (which uses SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP)
    and PASS on the "after" code (which uses SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG + AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    normalized = '\n'.join((line.rstrip() for line in source.splitlines()))
    assert 'AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP' in normalized, 'Expected azuread_map_groups to read AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP from SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG in the corrected implementation, but AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP was not found in /workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py.'

def test_azuread_group_mapping_settings_is_read_from_pipeline_config_not_legacy_setting__a3():
    """
    The fixed implementation no longer reads Azure AD group mapping from a legacy
    top-level Django setting SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP (via getattr/hasattr).
    Instead it requires SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG and reads AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP
    from that dict.

    This should FAIL on the "before" code (which uses SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP)
    and PASS on the "after" code (which uses SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG + AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    normalized = '\n'.join((line.rstrip() for line in source.splitlines()))
    assert 'SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP' not in normalized, 'azuread_map_groups should no longer reference the legacy SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP setting. It should read AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP from SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG instead.'

def test_azuread_map_groups_uses_config_get_for_group_map():
    """
    Ensure the group mapping is fetched from the pipeline config dict using .get(...).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    normalized = '\n'.join((line.rstrip() for line in source.splitlines()))
    assert re.search('group_mapping\\s*=\\s*config\\.get\\(\\s*"AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP"\\s*,', normalized), 'Expected pattern `group_mapping = config.get("AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP", ...)` in azuread_map_groups, indicating group mapping is sourced from SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG. This was not found.'

This guide explains how to configure single sign-on (SSO) support for NetBox using [Microsoft Azure Active Directory (AD)](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/services/active-directory/) as an authentication backend.

## Azure AD Configuration

### 1. Create a test user (optional)

Create a new user in AD to be used for testing. You can skip this step if you already have a suitable account created.

### 2. Create an app registration

Under the Azure Active Directory dashboard, navigate to **Add > App registration**.

![Add an app registration](../../media/authentication/azure_ad_add_app_registration.png)

Enter a name for the registration (e.g. "NetBox") and ensure that the "single tenant" option is selected.

Under "Redirect URI", select "Web" for the platform and enter the path to your NetBox installation, ending with `/oauth/complete/azuread-oauth2/`. Note that this URI **must** begin with `https://` unless you are referencing localhost (for development purposes).

![App registration parameters](../../media/authentication/azure_ad_app_registration.png)

Once finished, make note of the application (client) ID; this will be used when co...ser account has been replicated locally to NetBox, and can now be assigned groups and permissions within the NetBox admin UI.

## Troubleshooting

### Redirect URI does not Match

Azure requires that the authenticating client request a redirect URI that matches what you've configured for the app in step two. This URI **must** begin with `https://` (unless using `localhost` for the domain).

If Azure complains that the requested URI starts with `http://` (not HTTPS), it's likely that your HTTP server is misconfigured or sitting behind a load balancer, so NetBox is not aware that HTTPS is being use. To force the use of an HTTPS redirect URI, set `SOCIAL_AUTH_REDIRECT_IS_HTTPS = True` in `configuration.py` per the [python-social-auth docs](https://python-social-auth.readthedocs.io/en/latest/configuration/settings.html#processing-redirects-and-urlopen).

### Not Logged in After Authenticating

If you are redirected to the NetBox UI after authenticating successfully, but are _not_ logged in, double-check the configured backend and app registration. The instructions in this guide pertain only to the `azuread.AzureADOAuth2` backend using a single-tenant app registration.
'

import re

def test_azuread_map_groups_defines_logger_locally__a1():
    """
    Regression test for review comment: `logger` has not been defined.

    The "before" code calls `logger.info(...)` inside azuread_map_groups() without defining `logger`,
    which would raise NameError at runtime. The "after" code defines a local logger via
    logging.getLogger(...) inside azuread_map_groups().
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('(?ms)^def azuread_map_groups\\([^\\)]*\\):\\n(?P<body>(?:^[ \\t]+.*\\n)*)', source)
    assert m is not None, 'Expected authentication.py to define function azuread_map_groups().'

def test_azuread_map_groups_defines_logger_locally__a2():
    """
    Regression test for review comment: `logger` has not been defined.

    The "before" code calls `logger.info(...)` inside azuread_map_groups() without defining `logger`,
    which would raise NameError at runtime. The "after" code defines a local logger via
    logging.getLogger(...) inside azuread_map_groups().
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('(?ms)^def azuread_map_groups\\([^\\)]*\\):\\n(?P<body>(?:^[ \\t]+.*\\n)*)', source)
    body = m.group('body')
    assert 'logger = logging.getLogger(' in body, 'azuread_map_groups() must define a local logger (e.g., `logger = logging.getLogger(...)`) to avoid NameError when calling logger.info/logger.error.'

results.comment_text:
@user1:
We probably don't need this setting. Adding/removing this function to/from the authentication pipeline should be enough to enable or disable the functionality.
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_azuread_group_mapping_no_toggle_setting_gate__a1():
    """
    The review comment indicates we should not need a dedicated enable/disable setting.
    Instead, adding/removing the function from the social-auth pipeline should control usage.

    Therefore, azuread_map_groups should NOT be gated by a setting like
    SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_MAP_GROUP_PERMS (present in the "before" code).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py').read()
    m = re.search('\\ndef azuread_map_groups\\([^\\)]*\\):(?P<body>.*?)(?=\\n\\ndef |\\nclass |\\Z)', source, re.S)
    assert m, 'Expected to find def azuread_map_groups(...) in /workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py'

def test_azuread_group_mapping_no_toggle_setting_gate__a2():
    """
    The review comment indicates we should not need a dedicated enable/disable setting.
    Instead, adding/removing the function from the social-auth pipeline should control usage.

    Therefore, azuread_map_groups should NOT be gated by a setting like
    SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_MAP_GROUP_PERMS (present in the "before" code).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py').read()
    m = re.search('\\ndef azuread_map_groups\\([^\\)]*\\):(?P<body>.*?)(?=\\n\\ndef |\\nclass |\\Z)', source, re.S)
    body = m.group('body')
    assert 'SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_MAP_GROUP_PERMS' not in body, 'azuread_map_groups() should not be gated by SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_MAP_GROUP_PERMS; enable/disable should be controlled by adding/removing the pipeline function.'

import re

def test_azuread_map_groups_uses_hasattr_for_pipeline_config_guard__a1():
    """
    The review comment requests using:
        if not hasattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP"):
    (i.e., a hasattr()-based presence check rather than getattr(..., False) truthiness)
    for guarding configuration access.

    Since we cannot import Django modules here, enforce this via source inspection.
    This test is designed to fail on the "before" code (which uses getattr(..., False))
    and pass on the "after" code (which uses hasattr(settings, "...") for the config guard).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py').read()
    assert 'if not hasattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG")' in source, 'Expected azuread_map_groups to guard configuration using hasattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_PIPELINE_CONFIG") (presence check), rather than relying on getattr(..., False) truthiness.'

def test_azuread_map_groups_uses_hasattr_for_pipeline_config_guard__a2():
    """
    The review comment requests using:
        if not hasattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP"):
    (i.e., a hasattr()-based presence check rather than getattr(..., False) truthiness)
    for guarding configuration access.

    Since we cannot import Django modules here, enforce this via source inspection.
    This test is designed to fail on the "before" code (which uses getattr(..., False))
    and pass on the "after" code (which uses hasattr(settings, "...") for the config guard).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py').read()
    old_patterns = ['getattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP", False)', 'getattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP", False)']
    assert not any((p in source for p in old_patterns)), 'Found legacy getattr(..., False) truthiness checks for AzureAD mapping settings. These should be replaced by presence checks (hasattr or dict key existence) to avoid treating empty-but-defined configuration as undefined.'

def test_azuread_map_groups_uses_hasattr_for_pipeline_config_guard__a3():
    """
    The review comment requests using:
        if not hasattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP"):
    (i.e., a hasattr()-based presence check rather than getattr(..., False) truthiness)
    for guarding configuration access.

    Since we cannot import Django modules here, enforce this via source inspection.
    This test is designed to fail on the "before" code (which uses getattr(..., False))
    and pass on the "after" code (which uses hasattr(settings, "...") for the config guard).
    """
    source = open('/workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py').read()
    old_patterns = ['getattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP", False)', 'getattr(settings, "SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_GROUP_MAP", False)']
    assert re.search('\\ndef\\s+azuread_map_groups\\s*\\(', source), 'Expected azuread_map_groups() to exist in /workspace/netbox/netbox/authentication.py.'
